rxn_class_summary: Electrochemical oxidative 1,2-aryl migration coupling of allyl alcohols with sulfonyl hydrazides to access γ-keto sulfones via a radical-mediated semipinacol-type rearrangement
electrochemical_role: Anodic oxidation of sulfonyl hydrazide (2a) to a sulfonyl diazonium/radical species (loss of 3H+, 3e-, then N2 extrusion gives a sulfonyl radical); a later C-centered radical intermediate is further oxidized at the anode (-e-) to a cation that loses a proton to give the ketone product; protons are reduced at the cathode to H2

key_intermediates.name: sulfonyl diazonium intermediate A (ArSO2-N=N+) from anodic oxidation of TsNHNH2
sulfonyl radical B (ArSO2•) after N2 extrusion
C-centered tertiary carbinol radical C (sulfonyl radical added to C=C of allyl alcohol)
carbocation E (anodic oxidation of radical D)

electron_flow: Anode oxidizes the sulfonyl hydrazide (2a) with loss of 3H+/3e- to a diazonium species A, which extrudes N2 to give sulfonyl radical B; B adds to the C=C of allyl alcohol 1a, forming carbinol radical C, which undergoes 1,2-aryl migration to ketyl-type radical D; D is oxidized at the anode to cation E, deprotonation of which yields the γ-keto sulfone 3aa, while protons are reduced at the cathode to H2.

probe_reagents.name: TEMPO
probe_reagents.observation: Addition of 3.0 equiv TEMPO totally prevented the transformation (0% of 3aa), indicating a radical pathway

probe_reagents.name: BHT (butylated hydroxytoluene)
probe_reagents.observation: 3.0 equiv BHT greatly decreased conversion (3aa yield dropped to 36%), consistent with radical intermediates

probe_reagents.name: Cyclic voltammetry of 1a, 2a, and 1a+2a
probe_reagents.observation: 2a shows oxidation peaks at 1.50 V and 2.01 V vs Ag/AgCl while 1a oxidizes at 2.36 V; in the mixture the 1a peak shifts, indicating 2a is preferentially oxidized at the anode and interacts with 1a

probe_reagents.name: X-ray diffraction of product 4
probe_reagents.observation: Structure of spirocyclic sulfone 4 (no 1,2-migration product from rigid benzo-cycloheptane substrate) confirmed by single-crystal XRD (CCDC 2268921), supporting steric control of the migration pathway

notes: For rigid benzo-cycloheptane-substituted allyl alcohols, no 1,2-aryl migration occurs and the sulfonyl radical instead adds to the C=C to give spirocyclic/epoxide-type sulfones (products 4 and 5), attributed to steric hindrance preventing the aryl shift; this represents a divergent radical pathway off the main rearrangement mechanism.
